audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for tradition of elders, heart/defilement teaching, Canaanite woman, healing summary, and feeding four thousand.

verses.restored: Then Pharisees and scribes from Jerusalem came to Jesus, saying,
Why do your disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands when they eat bread.
He answered them, Why do you also transgress the command of the Divine because of your tradition?
For the Divine said, Honor father and mother, and, The one speaking evil of father or mother must surely come to an end.
But you say, Whoever says to father or mother, Whatever you might have gained from me is a gift-offering,
he need not honor his father. So you have made the word of the Divine void because of your tradition.
Hypocrites, Isaiah prophesied beautifully about you, saying:
This people honors me with lips, but their heart is far away from me.
In vain they worship me, teaching as teachings the commands of humans.
Calling the crowd to him, he said to them, Hear and understand:
Not what enters the mouth makes a person common/unclean, but what comes out of the mouth; this makes a person common/unclean.
Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this word?
He answered, Every plant that my heavenly Father did not plant will be uprooted.
Leave them; they are blind guides. If blind guide blind, both will fall into a pit.
Peter answered him, Explain this parable to us.
He said, Are you also still without understanding?
Do you not perceive that everything entering the mouth goes into the belly and is passed out?
But the things coming out of the mouth come out from the heart, and those make a person common/unclean.
For from the heart come harmful reasonings, murders, adulteries, sexual unfaithfulness, thefts, false witness, slanders.
These are what make a person common/unclean; but eating with unwashed hands does not make a person common/unclean.
Jesus went away from there and withdrew into the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
And look, a Canaanite woman from those borders came out and cried, Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David; my daughter is badly troubled.
But he did not answer her a word. His disciples came and urged him, saying, Release her, because she cries out after us.
He answered, I was not sent except to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
But she came and bowed/prostrated before him, saying, Lord, help me.
He answered, It is not beautiful/good to take the children’s bread and throw it to the little dogs.
She said, Yes, Lord; yet even the little dogs eat from the crumbs falling from their masters' table.
Then Jesus answered her, O woman, great is your trust; let it happen for you as you desire. And her daughter was healed from that hour.
Departing from there, Jesus came beside the Sea of Galilee, went up the mountain, and sat there.
Great crowds came to him, having with them the lame, blind, crippled, mute, and many others; they laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
So the crowd marveled, seeing mute people speaking, crippled people whole, lame people walking, and blind people seeing; and they glorified the Divine of Israel.

Jesus called his disciples and said, I am moved with compassion for the crowd, because they remain with me three days and have nothing to eat; I do not want to release them hungry, lest they faint on the way.
The disciples said to him, From where in a deserted place are there enough loaves for us to satisfy so great a crowd?
Jesus said to them, How many loaves do you have? They said, Seven, and a few little fish.
Commanding the crowd to recline on the ground,
he took the seven loaves and the fish, gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples to the crowds.
They all ate and were filled, and they took up what remained of the broken pieces, seven baskets full.
Those eating were four thousand men, besides women and children.
After releasing the crowds, he entered the boat and came into the borders of Magadan.
